perturbation.original_text: Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees may, at their discretion, set up any number of Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. However, there is no obligation to establish any Smaaash Centres within the first year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the franchisee set up at least 6 Smaaash Centres within the first year, creating a firm obligation. This perturbation replaces 'shall be under an obligation' with 'may, at their discretion', and adds a contradictory clause: 'there is no obligation to establish any Smaaash Centres within the first year'. This directly contradicts the initial requirement, introducing uncertainty regarding the franchisee's obligations and is contradictory in the location it's placed in. The contradiction centers on whether setting up Smaaash Centres is mandatory or optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees may, at their discretion, set up any number of Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. However, there is no obligation to establish any Smaaash Centres within the first year. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices. Franchisee shall perform its obligations hereunder without using subcontractors, sub-distributors, independent sales representatives, agents, Franchisee's affiliates or other non-employees ("Third Parties") to perform the obligations of Franchisee under this Agreement except to the contrary specifically stated in this Agreement or unless they have been approved, in writing, in advance, by Franchisor, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as it modifies a mandatory obligation ('shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6') to a discretionary option ('may, at their discretion... there is no obligation'). Since both texts are located in the same section, the change introduces a clear contradiction regarding the franchisee's obligation to set up Smaaash Centers, satisfying the structural flaw definition which says 'When contract sections contradict each other based on placement, it creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.'

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). Franchisee shall modify any disapproved Promotional Material to satisfy Franchisor's reasonable objections so that it is acceptable to Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's review, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). Franchisor's approval is not required; the materials are for informational purposes only, and Franchisee is not required to modify any Promotional Material based on Franchisor's feedback.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Franchisee to obtain the Franchisor's prior written approval for promotional materials, thereby granting the Franchisor control over marketing. This perturbation changes 'prior written approval' to 'review' and adds a contradictory clause: 'Franchisor's approval is not required', which nullifies the original control, stating that the Franchisee is not required to modify any Promotional Material. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding whether the Franchisee needs approval from the Franchisor for promotional materials and is directly contradictory in the section it is being placed in. The contradiction introduces uncertainty about the approval process of advertising material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's review, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). Franchisor's approval is not required; the materials are for informational purposes only, and Franchisee is not required to modify any Promotional Material based on Franchisor's feedback. Franchisor shall provide Franchisee with the creative elements of any Promotional Materials that Franchisor creates or acquires for use in connection with the advertising and sale of Products outside the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by explicitly stating that the Franchisor's approval is not required, and the Franchisee is not required to modify promotional material based on the Franchisor's feedback, while the original text implies that the Franchisor has the right to review and request changes to Promotional Material. The contradiction creates uncertainty on whether the Franchisee needs approval to move forward with advertisement, directly contradicting the obligations stated in the original text, making it difficult to enforce the section.
